features.summary: Broadcom reported record Q1 FY2022 revenue of $7.7 billion, up 16% year-over-year, driven by semiconductor solutions growing 20% to $5.9 billion and infrastructure software growing 5% to $1.8 billion. Enterprise demand recovery accelerated strongly while hyperscale customers upgraded data centers and service providers deployed next-generation fiber infrastructure. The company maintained extended lead times of 50 weeks with semiconductor backlog growing to over $25 billion and software backlog at over $15 billion.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q2 consolidated revenue of $7.9 billion with 20% year-over-year growth, driven by semiconductor revenue accelerating to 25% growth (35% excluding wireless). They have visibility through end of 2022 for both demand and supply, with first half of 2023 expected to remain strong. The company maintains confidence in long-term semiconductor industry growth of mid-single digits over the next decade, despite current elevated growth rates being unsustainable long-term.
features.risks: Extended lead times and supply constraints continue with no improvement, creating potential for inventory buildup in the channel. Material cost increases from wafers and substrates are being passed to customers, but pricing power may not be sustainable beyond first half of 2023. The wireless business remains heavily concentrated on one North American customer, creating revenue concentration risk despite the strategic partnership.
features.key_drivers: Strong enterprise IT spending recovery driving demand across networking, server storage, and broadband segments. Hyperscale deployment of next-generation products including Tomahawk 4 switches and custom AI/ML accelerators. New product launches with higher content and margins, including Wi-Fi 6E, DOCSIS 4.0, and advanced storage connectivity solutions. Infrastructure software showing stable recurring revenue with 121% renewal rates and growing annual recurring revenue of $5.3 billion.
